seoTitle: St James vs Stake Hill — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between St James and Stake Hill. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: St James vs Stake Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in St James and Stake Hill.
heading: St James vs Stake Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St James, Western Australia and Stake Hill, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St James has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Stake Hill (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St James or Stake Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St James has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Stake Hill scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St James scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Stake Hill scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St James or Stake Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St James has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
